concepts.name: Emergent gravity
concepts.definition: The thesis that the spacetime metric and its Einstein dynamics are not fundamental variables but a collective/effective, low-energy description of a deeper microscopic theory, analogous to how hydrodynamics or elasticity emerges from molecular physics.

concepts.name: Induced gravity (Sakharov)
concepts.definition: Sakharov's 1967 idea that the Einstein-Hilbert term arises as a one-loop effective action of quantum matter fields on a curved background; Newton's constant and the cosmological constant are determined by the UV cutoff and field spectrum (metric elasticity of the vacuum).

concepts.name: Metric elasticity
concepts.definition: Sakharov's analogy in which curvature shifts the zero-point energy of the vacuum, producing a stiffness of space proportional to the Ricci scalar R, just like stress in an elastic continuum.

concepts.name: Einstein equation as equation of state
concepts.definition: Jacobson's 1995 result that G_{munu}+Lambda g_{munu}=8 pi G T_{munu} is a macroscopic thermodynamic consistency condition (an equation of state) for horizon thermodynamics rather than a fundamental equation of motion.

concepts.name: Clausius relation delta Q = T dS
concepts.definition: Thermodynamic relation between heat flux across a horizon and entropy change; the engine of Jacobson's derivation when imposed for all local causal horizons.

concepts.name: Local Rindler / causal horizon
concepts.definition: For every point and null direction there exists a locally accelerating observer with a horizon, to which one assigns Unruh temperature T = hbar a / 2 pi c k_B and entropy S = eta A.

concepts.name: Unruh temperature
concepts.definition: T = hbar a / (2 pi c k_B): the effective thermal temperature of the vacuum seen by an observer with proper acceleration a; supplies the temperature in delta Q = T dS and underlies analogue Hawking radiation.

concepts.name: Bekenstein-Hawking entropy
concepts.definition: S = k_B c^3 A / (4 G hbar) = A / (4 l_P^2): horizon entropy proportional to area; the universal input assumption S proportional to A for the entire emergent/entropic program.

concepts.name: Holographic principle / screen
concepts.definition: Information about a bulk region is encoded on its boundary surface with N = A c^3 / (G hbar) bits; backbone of Verlinde's holographic screens and Padmanabhan's holographic equipartition.

concepts.name: Entropic force
concepts.definition: A macroscopic force F = T dS/dx driving a system toward higher-entropy configurations (like polymer elasticity); Verlinde's 2010 mechanism by which Newtonian gravity and Einstein equations are recovered.

concepts.name: Equipartition law
concepts.definition: E = (1/2) N k_B T: each surface (horizon) degree of freedom carries (1/2) k_B T of energy; used by Verlinde and Padmanabhan to define horizon temperature and recover Newton's law.

concepts.name: Holographic equipartition (Padmanabhan)
concepts.definition: In any static spacetime N_sur = N_bulk; the departure from this equality, dV/dt = L_P^2 (N_sur - N_bulk), drives cosmic expansion and reproduces the Friedmann equation.

concepts.name: Emergence of cosmic space
concepts.definition: Padmanabhan's proposal that the expansion of the universe is the quest of spacetime to reach holographic equipartition; the Hubble volume evolves so that surface and bulk degrees of freedom equalize.

concepts.name: CosMIn (cosmic information)
concepts.definition: A dimensionless conserved count of modes crossing the Hubble radius across the three cosmic epochs; the single postulate N = 4 pi reproduces the observed cosmological constant Lambda l_P^2 approx 3.4e-122.

concepts.name: Entanglement equilibrium (Jacobson 2015)
concepts.definition: Hypothesis that vacuum entanglement entropy of a small geodesic ball is maximal at fixed volume in a locally maximally symmetric state; stationarity of total entropy is equivalent to the semiclassical Einstein equation.

concepts.name: Modular Hamiltonian
concepts.definition: The generator K = H_zeta whose expectation equals the matter (IR) contribution to entanglement entropy; for CFTs delta<K> = (2 pi/hbar) delta<T_00>, central to entanglement equilibrium and first-law-of-entanglement derivations of gravity.

concepts.name: Volume-law entropy of de Sitter
concepts.definition: In de Sitter space the thermal entropy of the cosmological horizon, spread through the volume, gives a term proportional to V that overtakes the area law beyond the Hubble scale; source of Verlinde's extra dark gravity.

concepts.name: Apparent dark matter (Verlinde 2016)
concepts.definition: Not a particle but an elastic, memory-like response of the dark-energy entropy to baryonic matter; produces extra gravity reproducing flat rotation curves and MOND-like behaviour with a_0 = c H_0.

concepts.name: MOND (Modified Newtonian Dynamics)
concepts.definition: Empirical modification of gravity below an acceleration scale a_0 yielding flat rotation curves; Verlinde's emergent gravity reproduces the MOND formula for point masses but differs (factor ~2) for extended bodies.

concepts.name: Radial acceleration relation (RAR)
concepts.definition: Tight empirical correlation between observed and baryonic acceleration in galaxies with negligible intrinsic scatter; used by Lelli et al. to challenge Verlinde's emergent gravity, which predicts intrinsic thickness.

concepts.name: Analogue gravity
concepts.definition: Framework in which perturbations (sound, light, quasiparticles) in a moving fluid or BEC propagate on an emergent effective curved metric; lets laboratory systems mimic horizons and Hawking radiation.

concepts.name: Acoustic metric / dumb hole
concepts.definition: Unruh's effective metric for sound in a flowing fluid; a sonic (dumb-hole) horizon forms where flow speed equals sound speed |v| = c_s, with an associated analogue Hawking temperature from the surface gravity (velocity gradient).

concepts.name: Hawking radiation
concepts.definition: Thermal particle emission from a horizon at temperature set by surface gravity; observed in analogue (BEC) black holes by Steinhauer, confirming the kinematics (mode mixing, partner entanglement) but not the astrophysical effect itself.

concepts.name: Fermi point (Volovik)
concepts.definition: A topologically stable defect in momentum space (as in superfluid 3He-A) near which relativistic fields, gauge symmetry and effective gravity emerge from the quantum vacuum.

concepts.name: Gravity as hydrodynamics / geometro-hydrodynamics
concepts.definition: View (Hu, Volovik) that general relativity is the long-wavelength, low-energy hydrodynamic/kinetic limit of an underlying many-body quantum system, with semiclassical gravity one rung above.

concepts.name: Non-equilibrium spacetime thermodynamics
concepts.definition: Eling-Guedens-Jacobson 2006 extension where dS = delta Q/T + d_i S with an internal entropy production term (bulk/shear viscosity of the horizon), required to obtain f(R) gravity and interpreted as tidal heating.

concepts.name: Cosmological constant problem
concepts.definition: The ~120 order-of-magnitude mismatch between the vacuum energy predicted by QFT and the observed Lambda; reappears in induced gravity and is addressed (as a postulate) by CosMIn and Bianconi's G-field.

concepts.name: Gravity from quantum relative entropy (Bianconi)
concepts.definition: Bianconi's 2024/2025 action given by the quantum relative entropy between the spacetime metric and the metric induced by matter; an auxiliary G-field (Lagrange multipliers) yields a dressed Einstein-Hilbert action with an emergent small positive Lambda.

formulas.name: Jacobson Clausius relation for spacetime
formulas.latex: \delta Q = T\,dS,\qquad T=\frac{\hbar a}{2\pi c k_B},\qquad dS=\eta\,\delta A,\qquad \eta=\frac{k_B c^3}{4G\hbar}
formulas.meaning: Imposing the Clausius relation with Unruh temperature and entropy proportional to horizon area for all local Rindler horizons is the starting point of the thermodynamic derivation of the Einstein equation.

formulas.name: Einstein equation from delta Q = T dS
formulas.latex:
T_{\mu
u}k^\mu k^
u =  rac{\hbar\eta}{2\pi}R_{\mu
u}k^\mu k^
u \;\Rightarrow\; G_{\mu
u}+\Lambda g_{\mu
u}=8\pi G\,T_{\mu
u}
formulas.meaning: Requiring the Clausius relation to hold for every null direction at every point forces the full Einstein equation with a cosmological constant arising as an integration constant; G = 1/(4 hbar eta).

formulas.name: Non-equilibrium spacetime entropy balance
formulas.latex: dS = \frac{\delta Q}{T} + d_i S,\qquad d_i S \;\propto\; \int (\zeta\,\theta^2 + 2\eta_s\,\sigma^2)\,d\lambda\,dA
formulas.meaning: Equilibrium thermodynamics yields only Einstein gravity; obtaining f(R) and accounting for horizon shear requires an internal entropy-production term interpreted as bulk/shear viscosity of spacetime.

formulas.name: Sakharov induced gravitational action
formulas.meaning: Integrating out matter fields induces the Einstein-Hilbert term; the induced Newton constant is quadratically sensitive to the UV cutoff, implying a Planck-scale cutoff, but the sign and the induced Lambda are problematic.

formulas.name: Verlinde entropic-force derivation of Newton's law
formulas.latex: \Delta S = 2\pi k_B\frac{mc}{\hbar}\Delta x,\quad N=\frac{Ac^3}{G\hbar},\quad E=\tfrac12 N k_B T = Mc^2 \;\Rightarrow\; F=T\frac{\Delta S}{\Delta x}=\frac{GMm}{r^2}
formulas.meaning: Combining a holographic screen of N bits, equipartition of the enclosed energy Mc^2, and a Bekenstein-type entropy change recovers Newton's inverse-square law as an entropic force.

formulas.name: Padmanabhan emergence of cosmic space
formulas.meaning: Cosmic expansion is the drive toward holographic equipartition N_sur = N_bulk; this single equation reproduces the standard Friedmann equation.

formulas.name: CosMIn value of the cosmological constant
formulas.meaning: Postulating that the conserved cosmic information equals 4 pi (bits on a unit sphere) yields the observed numerical value of the cosmological constant.

formulas.name: Entanglement equilibrium stationarity
formulas.meaning: Stationarity of total (UV area + IR modular) entanglement entropy of a small causal diamond at fixed volume is equivalent to the semiclassical Einstein equation G_ab + Lambda g_ab = 8 pi G delta<T_ab>.

formulas.name: Verlinde apparent dark matter relation
formulas.meaning: Relates the apparent (emergent) dark mass M_D to the enclosed baryonic mass M_B and the Hubble acceleration a_0 = c H_0; for point masses it reduces to the MOND-like sqrt(a_0 a_N) law, with no free parameters.

formulas.name: Unruh acoustic metric
formulas.latex: ds^2=\frac{\rho}{c_s}\Big[-(c_s^2-v^2)dt^2-2\,\vec v\cdot d\vec x\,dt+d\vec x\cdot d\vec x\Big]
formulas.meaning: Sound waves in a flowing fluid of density rho, sound speed c_s and velocity v propagate on this effective Lorentzian metric; a horizon forms where |v| = c_s, with analogue Hawking temperature T_H = hbar kappa / (2 pi k_B).

formulas.name: Analogue Hawking temperature
formulas.meaning: Thermal temperature of analogue Hawking radiation set by the analogue surface gravity kappa (velocity gradient at the sonic horizon); measured to match the spectrum's thermality by Steinhauer's group.

references.title: Vacuum quantum fluctuations in curved space and the theory of gravitation
references.significance: Founding paper of induced gravity: gravity as metric elasticity of the vacuum.

references.title: Experimental Black-Hole Evaporation?
references.significance: Introduced the dumb hole and the acoustic analogue of Hawking radiation, founding analogue gravity.

references.title: Thermodynamics of Spacetime: The Einstein Equation of State
references.significance: Cornerstone result: derives the Einstein equation from delta Q = T dS on local Rindler horizons.

references.title: Sakharov's induced gravity: a modern perspective
references.significance: Authoritative modern review of induced gravity and its sign/cosmological-constant problems.

references.title: The Universe in a Helium Droplet
references.significance: Develops the Fermi-point scenario: emergence of relativistic fields, gauge fields and gravity from the quantum vacuum of superfluid 3He.

references.authors: C. Eling, R. Guedens, T. Jacobson
references.title: Non-equilibrium Thermodynamics of Spacetime
references.significance: Shows f(R) gravity and horizon shear require internal entropy production (spacetime viscosity).

references.title: On the Origin of Gravity and the Laws of Newton
references.significance: Most-cited entropic-gravity paper: derives Newton's law and Einstein equations as an entropic force.

references.title: Gravity is not an entropic force
references.significance: Key experimental objection: neutron bound-state (qBounce) data argued to disprove entropic origin of gravity.

references.authors: C. Barcelo, S. Liberati, M. Visser
references.title: Analogue Gravity
references.significance: Standard Living Reviews survey of analogue gravity, acoustic metrics, and what analogues can and cannot show.

references.title: Gravitation: Foundations and Frontiers
references.significance: Textbook articulation of the thermodynamic/emergent gravity program and holographic action structure.

references.title: Emergence and Expansion of Cosmic Space as due to the Quest for Holographic Equipartition
references.significance: Derives cosmic expansion (Friedmann equation) from dV/dt = N_sur - N_bulk.

references.title: CosMIn: The Solution to the Cosmological Constant Problem
references.significance: Postulate N = 4 pi yields the observed Lambda l_P^2 approx 3.4e-122, a rare quantitative output.

references.authors: T. Faulkner, M. Guica, T. Hartman, R. C. Myers, M. Van Raamsdonk
references.title: Gravitation from Entanglement in Holographic CFTs
references.significance: Linearized Einstein equations from the first law of entanglement entropy; the AdS/CFT analogue of Jacobson's theorem.

references.title: Entanglement Equilibrium and the Einstein Equation
references.significance: Derives semiclassical Einstein equation from maximal/stationary vacuum entanglement entropy of small causal diamonds.

references.title: Observation of quantum Hawking radiation and its entanglement in an analogue black hole
references.significance: First reported observation of spontaneous, entangled Hawking radiation in a BEC analogue black hole.

references.title: Emergent Gravity and the Dark Universe
references.significance: Derives apparent dark matter from de Sitter volume-law entropy with a_0 = c H_0; MOND-like with no free parameters.

references.title: First test of Verlinde's theory of Emergent Gravity using Weak Gravitational Lensing measurements
references.significance: Galaxy-galaxy lensing of 33,613 galaxies (KiDS+GAMA) consistent with Verlinde's parameter-free prediction.

references.authors: F. Lelli, S. S. McGaugh, J. M. Schombert, M. S. Pawlowski
references.title: Testing Verlinde's Emergent Gravity with the Radial Acceleration Relation
references.significance: Finds emergent gravity inconsistent with the tight, scatter-free radial acceleration relation.

references.authors: R. Dardashti, S. Hartmann, K. P. Y. Thebault, E. Winsberg
references.title: Hawking radiation and analogue experiments: A Bayesian analysis
references.significance: Argues via universality that analogue experiments can confirm Hawking radiation in real black holes.

references.authors: K. Crowther, N. S. Linnemann, C. Wuthrich
references.title: What we cannot learn from analogue experiments
references.significance: Counterargument that analogue confirmation of inaccessible targets begs the question (epistemic limits).

references.title: Bulk Entanglement Gravity without a Boundary: Towards Finding Einstein's Equation in Hilbert Space
references.significance: Recovers space and weak-field Einstein equation from bulk entanglement (space from Hilbert space).

references.authors: J. R. Munoz de Nova, K. Golubkov, V. I. Kolobov, J. Steinhauer
references.title: Observation of thermal Hawking radiation at the Hawking temperature in an analogue black hole
references.significance: Measured spectrum is thermal at the analogue Hawking temperature set by surface gravity.

references.title: Verlinde's emergent gravity versus MOND and the case of dwarf spheroidals
references.significance: Shows emergent gravity equals MOND for point masses but differs by a factor 2 in galaxy cores; tension with dwarfs.

references.title: Out of Nowhere: The Emergence of Spacetime in Quantum Theories of Gravity
references.significance: Philosophical analysis of spacetime emergence and the empirical-coherence problem across QG approaches.

references.authors: P. Svancara, S. Patrick, R. Gregory, C. F. Barenghi, S. Weinfurtner et al.
references.title: Rotating curved spacetime signatures from a giant quantum vortex
references.significance: Stationary giant quantum vortex in superfluid 4He simulates rotating black-hole ringdown and bound states.

references.title: Gravity from entropy
references.significance: Derives a dressed Einstein-Hilbert action with emergent small positive Lambda from quantum relative entropy of metrics; G-field.

references.title: Conservative entropic forces (Comments on Verlinde)
references.significance: Shows reproducing conservative Newtonian forces entropically demands unphysical entropy and many temperature baths.

references.authors: A. J. Schimmoller, G. McCaul, H. Abele, D. I. Bondar
references.title: Decoherence-Free Entropic Gravity: Model and Experimental Tests
references.significance: Open-quantum-system model of entropic gravity compatible with qBounce ultracold-neutron data.

references.title: General Relativity as Geometro-Hydrodynamics
references.significance: Articulates the kinetic-theory/hydrodynamic hierarchy in which GR and semiclassical gravity emerge.

openProblems.statement: Identify the microscopic degrees of freedom ('atoms of spacetime') whose statistical mechanics reproduces S = A/4 l_P^2 and the Einstein equation.
openProblems.whyHard: The thermodynamic derivations give only an equation of state, which is by construction insensitive to microscopic details; horizon entropy is universal and observer-dependent (Rindler), making the inverse problem ill-posed.
openProblems.attempts: LQG spin-network counting, causal-set link counting (Dou-Sorkin), induced-gravity field content, and entanglement-based 'it from qubit' programs each supply candidate microstates but none is established as THE microtheory.

openProblems.statement: Show that Sakharov-style integrating-out of matter gives the correct sign and magnitude of Newton's constant while not generating a huge cosmological constant.
openProblems.whyHard: 1/G_ind ~ Lambda_UV^2 and Lambda_0 ~ Lambda_UV^4 depend on a delicate boson/fermion balance at the UV cutoff where the effective theory breaks down; the sign can come out wrong.
openProblems.attempts: Supersymmetric cancellations, specific field-content tuning, and reformulations on AdS backgrounds; CosMIn and Bianconi's G-field address Lambda but by postulate.

openProblems.statement: Determine when spacetime is 'in equilibrium' (reversible delta Q = T dS giving Einstein) versus when internal entropy production (giving f(R)/viscosity) is required.
openProblems.whyHard: The boundary between equilibrium and non-equilibrium thermodynamics of horizons is imposed by hand and lacks a microscopic justification.
openProblems.attempts: Eling-Guedens-Jacobson bulk/shear viscosity terms; Chirco-Liberati origin-of-entropy-production analyses; still no first-principles criterion.

openProblems.statement: Provide a fully covariant, Lagrangian formulation of Verlinde's 2016 emergent gravity that also fits galaxy clusters and respects solar-system constraints.
openProblems.whyHard: The de Sitter volume-law entropy and 'elastic' response are heuristic, mix relativistic (Mc^2) and non-relativistic notions, lack a variational principle, and underpredict cluster cores by a factor of 2-3.
openProblems.attempts: Numerous phenomenological fits (Brouwer pass, Lelli/Hodson-Zhao fail); Hossenfelder's covariant version of a Verlinde-like force; no complete theory yet.

openProblems.statement: Reconcile an entropic/statistical origin of gravity with the observed quantum coherence of gravitationally bound states (qBounce, neutron interferometry).
openProblems.whyHard: Genuinely entropic forces should be noisy and induce decoherence that ultracold-neutron experiments do not see.
openProblems.attempts: Kobakhidze's no-go; rebuttals by Chaichian-Oksanen-Tureanu; decoherence-free open-system models (TU Wien) that evade it with extra assumptions.

openProblems.statement: Determine what, if anything, analogue Hawking radiation establishes about astrophysical black holes and trans-Planckian robustness.
openProblems.whyHard: The astrophysical target is experimentally inaccessible; inferring it from analogues requires assuming the modelling framework's adequacy, which critics call question-begging.
openProblems.attempts: Dardashti et al. Bayesian universality arguments for confirmation; Crowther-Linnemann-Wuthrich circularity counterargument; unresolved.

openProblems.statement: Establish whether the emergent/thermodynamic picture survives beyond the semiclassical regime and how it meshes with theories that quantize the metric.
openProblems.whyHard: Horizon thermodynamics is intrinsically semiclassical; the emergent program and the quantization program make opposite assumptions about what is fundamental.
openProblems.attempts: Entanglement-equilibrium and bulk-entanglement-gravity push toward Hilbert-space formulations; connections to AdS/CFT exist but a full quantum completion does not.

openProblems.statement: Explain the origin and validity of a generalized second law and the thermodynamic arrow of time within emergent gravity.
openProblems.whyHard: If gravity is thermodynamic, the second law should be derivable, but horizon entropy is observer-dependent and lacks an agreed microscopic ensemble.
openProblems.attempts: Generalized second law proofs (Wall), Jacobson-Parentani analyses; remains conceptually open in the emergent framework.

connections.description: Both rest on area-law entanglement entropy and the holographic principle. The first law of entanglement in AdS/CFT (Faulkner et al. 2014) reproduces linearized Einstein equations in the bulk, the holographic version of Jacobson's theorem; Verlinde 2016 explicitly builds on AdS area law and its breakdown in de Sitter. AdS/CFT supplies the concrete microtheory the generic emergent program lacks.

Jacobson's entanglement equilibrium (2015) and Cao-Carroll (2018) derive geometry and Einstein's equation directly from entanglement entropy and the modular Hamiltonian, sharing the whole 'spacetime from entanglement' / it-from-qubit apparatus (RT formula, modular flow). Less explored beyond linear order and without an AdS boundary.

The program takes Bekenstein-Hawking entropy and Hawking temperature as inputs and suggests the information paradox is a semiclassical artifact resolved by spacetime atoms; analogue black holes test Hawking kinematics and partner-mode entanglement, touching but not resolving the paradox (no Page curve from emergent gravity itself).

Entanglement equilibrium yields the semiclassical equation G_ab = 8 pi G <T_ab>; the whole thermodynamic program lives in the QFT-on-curved-background regime (Unruh, Hawking). Emergent gravity essentially claims semiclassical gravity IS the thermodynamic limit, so this is its home regime.

Uniquely test-rich: Verlinde 2016 is probed by galaxy-galaxy lensing (Brouwer, pass), rotation curves and RAR (Lelli, fail), dwarf spheroidals (Hodson-Zhao); entropic gravity by neutron interferometry/qBounce (Kobakhidze); and analogue gravity is the only laboratory-accessible part (Steinhauer, Weinfurtner).

Induced gravity and asymptotic safety both treat G and Lambda as effective/running quantities fixed by UV physics. Conjectured bridge: an AS UV fixed point could be the microtheory whose effective description is the induced EH action. Essentially nobody has systematically developed this link - prime 'barely explored' territory.

Both share the thermodynamic-statistical ethos: causal-set horizon entropy from counting causal links (Dou-Sorkin) gives S proportional to A, and Jacobson's local causal horizons have a natural discrete counterpart. Conceptual bridge exists; whether causal-set counting quantitatively reproduces Jacobson's derivation is open.

Philosophically opposed (LQG quantizes geometry; emergent gravity says geometry is not fundamental), yet potentially complementary: LQG reproduces S = A/4 from spin-network counting and could supply the 'atoms of spacetime' for the thermodynamic limit. Cross-development is almost nonexistent.

GFT condensate cosmology treats the universe as a quantum fluid of atoms of space, a literal realization of 'gravity as hydrodynamics' (Hu, Volovik); its emergent Friedmann equation parallels Padmanabhan's dV/dt = N_sur - N_bulk. Conceptually close, quantitatively unconnected.

Verlinde 2016 and CosMIn tie Lambda to other scales; swampland de Sitter/distance conjectures likewise place small positive Lambda at the edge of consistency. If Lambda is emergent (Bianconi G-field), it might illuminate the de Sitter swampland problem. Speculative and unworked.

Padmanabhan's emergence of cosmic space reformulates the Friedmann equation as a holographic-equipartition flow and is being generalized (FRW, Lanczos-Lovelock, deformed entropy). Connection to deeper quantum cosmology (Wheeler-DeWitt) is weak.

The same one-loop integration that induces G generates a huge vacuum energy; CosMIn (N = 4 pi) and Bianconi's G-field both yield the observed small Lambda but as postulates. The emergent framework reframes, and occasionally numerically hits, the CC problem rather than solving it from first principles.

Minimal-length/UV-cutoff structures central to induced gravity (Lambda_UV setting G) resonate with noncommutative spacetime's built-in length scale; both regularize horizon entropy counting. Almost no direct work links Jacobson/Sakharov thermodynamics to NCG spectral-action gravity.
